insights.title: Intentional Erasure
insights.summary: Missing traces can indicate deliberate removal rather than absence of contact.
insights.transfer_rule: Absence of evidence can itself be evidence when the context expects traces.

cases.title: The Fingerprint Paradox
cases.summary: A frequently handled object has no fingerprints at all. The lack of trace is itself the puzzle.
evidence: A frequently used metal object shows no visible fingerprints.
The object sits in a high-contact location.
There are no signs that the object was recently replaced.
detective_questions: What should normally be present on such an object?
If a trace is absent, what mechanisms could explain that absence?
Does the absence point to neglect, replacement, or deliberate cleaning?
analysis_protocol: Record only what is present or absent in the evidence.
List multiple explanations for the missing trace.
Prefer the explanation that best fits expected baseline behavior.

evidence_meta.text: A frequently used metal object shows no visible fingerprints.
The object sits in a high-contact location.
There are no signs that the object was recently replaced.

cases.hidden_truth: The missing fingerprints most likely reflect intentional removal.
cases.false_narrative: No prints means no one touched the object.

contradictions.description: Frequent use should leave prints, yet none exist.

inference_traps.description: No fingerprints does not mean no one touched the object; expected traces may have been removed.
trap_keywords: absence of prints means no contact

inference_traps.description: Natural fading is a weak explanation for a frequently handled high-contact object with no replacement signs.

required_concept_rules.name: Expected baseline
required_concept_rules.description: Notes that a frequently used object should carry prints.

required_concept_rules.name: Negative evidence as signal
required_concept_rules.description: Treats absence of trace as active signal, not neutral silence.

forbidden_patterns.description: Attributing missing prints to natural decay.
Assuming absence of prints means no contact occurred.
